activeSlice.name: winui-windowed-mods-and-lore-package-truth-cleanup
activeSlice.goal: make dynamic patch rows easier for agents/UIA to target, keep maintainer proof jargon out of normal Windowed & Mods copy, and make Lore/Ghidra package boundaries explicit
activeSlice.claimBoundary: WinUI UI/testability, package-truth, and documentation cleanup only; no BEA launch, no CDB attach, no byte patch, no Ghidra mutation, no Host/Join enablement, no online proof, no full offline Lore content-pack implementation, no release publication, and no installed-game mutation
latestFindings: Dynamic visible patch rows now expose RowAutomationId, CheckBoxAutomationId, and DetailsAutomationId derived from BinaryPatchSpec.Key.
The automation-id suffix preserves letters, digits, underscores, and hyphens instead of collapsing every non-alphanumeric character to underscore.

Normal debug-camera, online-unavailable, music-staging, and preset-detail copy now uses player-facing language and keeps CDB/proof-boundary/key-census/raw-offset/evidence jargon out of the primary path.
Codex read-only/adversarial consults reviewed the automation-id and copy boundaries; accepted findings were folded into tests and implementation.
Packaged Lore now distinguishes offline chapters from GitHub source links in visible WinUI copy and rendered Markdown badges.
ZIP package probe now rejects stale packaged Lore copy that claims all internal links stay in app while unbundled source links are externalized.
Consult consensus rejects raw full lore-book mirroring in the app ZIP because of Explorer path-length risk; full offline Lore should use a generated short-path content pack.
Live Ghidra project path is documented as C:\Users\david\Ghidra\Projects\BEA.gpr with store C:\Users\david\Ghidra\Projects\BEA.rep\; no Ghidra DB is tracked.
